# Preface {.unnumbered}

This book provides complete, executable solutions to all **green questions** (coding exercises) from:

> **Credible Answers to Hard Questions: Differences-in-Differences for Natural Experiments**
> by Clément de Chaisemartin & Xavier D'Haultfœuille (Princeton University Press, forthcoming)

Every exercise is solved in **three programming languages** — Stata, R, and Python — and the code **executes directly** inside this book, producing outputs, tables, and graphs so the reader can verify that results are exactly the same across all three languages.

Each chapter is presented in three separate pages, one per language, so that all outputs are generated live.

## Datasets

| Chapters | Paper                    | Stata (.dta) | R (.RData) | Python (.parquet) |
|----------|--------------------------|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| 3 & 4    | Moser & Voena (2012)     | [.dta](https://github.com/Credible-Answers/did_book/raw/main/cc_xd_didtextbook_2025_9_30/Data%20sets/Moser%20and%20Voena%202012/moser_voena_didtextbook.dta) | [.RData](https://github.com/Credible-Answers/did_book/raw/main/cc_xd_didtextbook_2025_9_30/Data%20sets/Moser%20and%20Voena%202012/moser_voena_didtextbook.RData) | [.parquet](https://github.com/Credible-Answers/did_book/raw/main/cc_xd_didtextbook_2025_9_30/Data%20sets/Moser%20and%20Voena%202012/moser_voena_didtextbook.parquet) |
| 5 & 8    | Gentzkow et al. (2011)   | [.dta](https://github.com/Credible-Answers/did_book/raw/main/cc_xd_didtextbook_2025_9_30/Data%20sets/Gentzkow%20Shapiro%20and%20Sinkinson%202011/gentzkowetal_didtextbook.dta) | [.RData](https://github.com/Credible-Answers/did_book/raw/main/cc_xd_didtextbook_2025_9_30/Data%20sets/Gentzkow%20Shapiro%20and%20Sinkinson%202011/gentzkowetal_didtextbook.RData) | [.parquet](https://github.com/Credible-Answers/did_book/raw/main/cc_xd_didtextbook_2025_9_30/Data%20sets/Gentzkow%20Shapiro%20and%20Sinkinson%202011/gentzkowetal_didtextbook.parquet) |
| 6        | Wolfers (2006)           | [.dta](https://github.com/Credible-Answers/did_book/raw/main/cc_xd_didtextbook_2025_9_30/Data%20sets/Wolfers%202006/wolfers2006_didtextbook.dta) | [.RData](https://github.com/Credible-Answers/did_book/raw/main/cc_xd_didtextbook_2025_9_30/Data%20sets/Wolfers%202006/wolfers2006_didtextbook.RData) | [.parquet](https://github.com/Credible-Answers/did_book/raw/main/cc_xd_didtextbook_2025_9_30/Data%20sets/Wolfers%202006/wolfers2006_didtextbook.parquet) |
| 7        | Pierce & Schott (2016)   | [.dta](https://github.com/Credible-Answers/did_book/raw/main/cc_xd_didtextbook_2025_9_30/Data%20sets/Pierce%20and%20Schott%202016/pierce_schott_didtextbook.dta) | [.RData](https://github.com/Credible-Answers/did_book/raw/main/cc_xd_didtextbook_2025_9_30/Data%20sets/Pierce%20and%20Schott%202016/pierce_schott_didtextbook.RData) | [.parquet](https://github.com/Credible-Answers/did_book/raw/main/cc_xd_didtextbook_2025_9_30/Data%20sets/Pierce%20and%20Schott%202016/pierce_schott_didtextbook.parquet) |

Download the dataset in your preferred format and place it in your working directory. All code blocks in the chapters assume the dataset is in the current directory.

## Requirements

- **Quarto** ≥ 1.4: <https://quarto.org/docs/download/>
- **Stata 17+** with `nbstata`: `pip install nbstata && python -m nbstata.install`
- **R** with packages: `install.packages(c("haven", "fixest", "dplyr", "ggplot2", "broom", "car"))`
- **Python** with packages: `pip install pandas numpy pyfixest scipy matplotlib pyreadstat`

## Source code

<https://github.com/Credible-Answers/did_book>

## Contributors

- [Anthony Quispe](https://github.com/anzonyquispe)
- [Karla Vega](https://karlavegaleg.github.io)
- [Joshbel Damian](https://damianrojasjhosbel-sudo.github.io/)
